qa_pairs.question: How do I upload or download files to Alliance Nextcloud using the `curl` command-line tool?
qa_pairs.answer: To upload a file to Alliance Nextcloud using `curl`, use the command `curl -k -u <username> -T <filename> https://nextcloud.computecanada.ca/remote.php/webdav/`. To download a file, use `curl -k -u <username> https://nextcloud.computecanada.ca/remote.php/webdav/<filename> -o <filename>`. Remember to replace `<username>` with your Alliance username and `<filename>` with the appropriate file path.

qa_pairs.question: What are the advantages of using `rclone` for managing files on Alliance Nextcloud compared to `curl`, and how is it configured?
qa_pairs.answer: `rclone` allows you to create a configuration profile for Alliance Nextcloud once, storing your password encrypted, so you don't need to re-enter service details repeatedly, unlike `curl`. To configure `rclone`, run `rclone config`, choose to create a new remote (n), name it (e.g., 'nextcloud'), select '52 / WebDAV' as the storage type, and provide `https://nextcloud.computecanada.ca/remote.php/dav/files/<your CCDB username>` as the URL. Specify '2 / Nextcloud' for the service, enter your CCDB username and password, then save the configuration. `rclone` is pre-installed on Alliance clusters.

qa_pairs.question: How can I share files or directories with other users or groups using Alliance Nextcloud?
qa_pairs.answer: When you select a file or directory to share in Alliance Nextcloud, you can type the user’s first name, last name, or username; a list of matched users registered in CCDB will appear. For sharing with groups, use their CCDB group name (e.g., default, RPP, RRG). To share with individuals who do not have an Alliance account, use the "Share link" option and provide their email address; Nextcloud will then send an email notification with a link to access the file.
